bids.reasoning: It is the very beginning of the negotiation (t=0.0) and the opponent has opened with an offer that yields my lowest possible utility on all issues. I will counter by asserting my ideal preferences to establish a strong starting position, proposing the maximum utility for myself across the board.

The negotiation is in its very early stages (t=0.02). The opponent demonstrated a willingness to concede on my most important issue (issueA) by jumping from valueD to valueB. To reciprocate this movement and encourage further concessions, I will make a small concession on my least important issue (issueD), lowering its value slightly from valueA to valueB, while holding firm on my top priorities to signal their importance. This maintains my overall utility at approximately 0.973.

The opposing party slightly backtracked on their previous concession regarding `issueA`, perhaps testing my persistence on it. Given the early stage of the negotiation (t=0.04), I will maintain my requests for my highest-weight issues (`issueA`, `issueB`, and `issueC`) at their maximum utilities. I will, however, fully concede on `issueD`, my least important issue, matches their previous offers, to continue showing a willingness to compromise and hopefully prompt a real concession on my key issues.

The opponent has made a significant concession on my most critical issue (issueA) by proposing valueA. Given that it is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.06), I want to reinforce this positive movement by locking in issueA while making a minor concession on issueC to explore potential tradeoffs. I will hold firm on issueB for now since it is my second most important issue.

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.08). The opponent has repeated their previous offer of {A: valueA, B: valueC, C: valueD, D: valueD}. They've conceded issueA to my highest-utility value, which is my most important issue (weight 0.4), but are holding firm on the other issues. I will accept their proposal on issueA and maintain my concession on issueD, while safely conceding slightly on issueB to 'valueB' to see if this brings us closer to a deal without severely compromising my utility.

The opponent has made significant concessions on my highest priority issues (issueA to 'valueA' and issueB to 'valueB') early in the game (t=0.10). Their persistence on issueC and issueD indicates these are likely their most heavily weighted issues. Since I have already conceded on issueD (my lowest priority), I will maintain the agreement on issues A, B, and D, and make a partial concession on issueC to 'valueB' to continue moving toward a mutually beneficial agreement while preserving a strong utility.

The time is still very early (t=0.12), and the opponent has made steady concessions, yielding my highest preference on issueA and a decent preference on issueB, while maintaining their apparent strong preference for valueD on issueD and valueC on issueC. I am targeting a utility around 0.84 to see if they are willing to concede more on issueC, while securing my strong utility on the more heavily weighted issues. I will hold firm on issueA and issueB, demand a better value on issueC, but afford them valueD on issueD.

The current time is early (t=0.14), but the opponent has shown a willingness to concede on issue A, issue B, and somewhat on issue C. Their latest offer (issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueD) gives me a utility of 0.733. I will return to my previous bid from t=0.10, conceding slightly on issue C compared to my last round, to maintain a high utility of 0.785 while keeping the pressure on them to bridge the gap on issue C.

The opponent is holding firm on issueC as valueC and issueD as valueD, having already made significant concessions on issueA and issueB. Since we are still very early in the negotiation (t=0.16) and their current offer gives me a utility of 0.733, I am going to explore a different tradeoff. I will concede issueC to their requested valueC, but request valueA for issueB to maintain my utility around 0.81.

My previous strategy was to explore tradeoffs on issueB and issueC. The opponent has held firm at issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueC, and issueD: valueD for multiple rounds, which gives me a utility of 0.733. Since we are still very early in the negotiation (t=0.18), I will push back slightly on issueC to see if they are willing to concede further, offering valueB on issueC which would raise my utility to 0.785.

The opponent is holding firm on their offer of `issueA: valueA`, `issueB: valueB`, `issueC: valueC`, `issueD: valueD`, having repeated it multiple times. While their offer provides reasonable utility (0.733), at `t=0.2` there is still plenty of time to explore better compromises. At time t=0.22, I have the time to negotiate for slightly higher utility on the less heavily weighted issues to better map out the opponent's flexibility, so I will counter by asking for 'valueB' on issueC and trying out a slight variance on issueD.

While this offer is acceptable as a fallback (utility 0.733), there is plenty of time left before the deadline (t=0.24).
